title: Frequently confused words: to/two/too
description: Can you tell the difference between the words "to," "two," and "too"?

assessment_items.question_content:
**Identify whether the word _to_, _two_, or _too_ is the appropriate word in this sentence.**

>  The soup was \_\_\_\_\_\_ hot for eating yet.



[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
People often confuse the words "to", "too", and "two":

* **to** is a preposition that shows *direction or transition*

 *I drive **to** work each morning.*

* **too** is an adverb that can mean "also" or an *excess of something*

 *She drank **too **much orange juice and didn't feel well.*

* **two** is the written version of the number 2

 *Bob Dylan and Paul Simon are **two** of the most successful songwriters of all time.*
This sentence needs to show *excess*, so we should use **too**.
